content.text: Now let me see each Tooltip usage in context:

</GridItem>

            <GridItem span={1}>
              <FormGroup className={graphFindStyle}>
                <GraphFindOptions kind="find" onSelect={this.updateFindOption} />
                {this.props.findValue && (
                  <Tooltip key="ot_clear_find" position="top" content="Clear Find...">
                    <Button
                      className={buttonClearStyle}
                      variant={ButtonVariant.control}
                      onClick={() => this.setFind('')}
                    >
                      <KialiIcon.Close />
                    </Button>
                  </Tooltip>
                )}
              </FormGroup>
            </GridItem>

            <GridItem span={5}>
              <FormGroup>
                <TextInput
                  id="graph_hide"
                  name="graph_hide"
                  ref={ref => {
                    this.hideInputRef = ref;
                  }}
                  autoComplete="on"
                  validated={isValid(this.state.hideInputValue ? !this.state.hideError : undefined)}
                  type="text"
                  onChange={(_event, val) => this.updateHide(val)}
                  defaultValue={this.state.hideInputValue}
                  onKeyDownCapture={this.checkSpecialKeyHide}
                  placeholder="Hide..."
                />
                {this.state.hideError && (
                  <FormHelperText>
                    <HelperText>
                      <HelperTextItem variant={'error'}>{this.state.hideError}</HelperTextItem>
                    </HelperText>
                  </FormHelperText>
                )}
              </FormGroup>
            </GridItem>

            <GridItem span={1}>
              <FormGroup className={graphHideStyle}>
                <GraphFindOptions kind="hide" onSelect={this.updateHideOption} />
                {this.props.hideValue && (
                  <Tooltip key="ot_clear_hide" position="top" content="Clear Hide...">
                    <Button
                      className={buttonClearStyle}
                      variant={ButtonVariant.control}
                      onClick={() => this.setHide('')}
                    >
                      <KialiIcon.Close />
                    </Button>
                  </Tooltip>
                )}
              </FormGroup>
            </GridItem>
          </Grid>
        </Form>

        {this.props.showFindHelp ? (
          <GraphHelpFind onClose={this.toggleFindHelp}>
            <Button
              data-test="graph-find-hide-help-button"
              variant={ButtonVariant.link}
              className={findHideHelpStyle}
              onClick={this.toggleFindHelp}
            >
              <KialiIcon.Info className={infoStyle} />
            </Button>
          </GraphHelpFind>
        ) : (
          <Tooltip key={'ot_graph_find_help'} position="top" content="Click to open Find/Hide help">
            <Button
              data-test="graph-find-hide-help-button"
              variant={ButtonVariant.link}
              className={findHideHelpStyle}
              onClick={this.toggleFindHelp}
            >
              <KialiIcon.Info className={infoStyle} />
            </Button>
          </Tooltip>
        )}
      </TourStop>
    );
  }

content.text:
Now let me reason through this:

**Migration rule analysis:**
- The rule is about Tooltip's transitive behavioral change: Popper's `appendTo` default changed from `'inline'` to `() => document.body` in PF v6.
- This means Tooltip content now renders into a portal (appended to `document.body`) by default instead of inline.
- This is a **behavioral change** — the Tooltip component itself hasn't changed its API, but its rendering behavior changed.

**Impact on the main file:**
- The Tooltips in this file are used as simple wrappers around buttons to show hover text ("Clear Find...", "Clear Hide...", "Click to open Find/Hide help").
- These are standard tooltip usages. The portal change means tooltip content renders in `document.body` instead of inline, which is typically fine for production use — tooltips are meant to overlay and portaling is actually the preferred behavior.
- **No code change is needed in the main file** for production functionality. The Tooltip import and usage are correct. The behavioral change (portal rendering) doesn't break the component's functionality — it changes WHERE the tooltip popup appears in the DOM, but visually it works the same.

**Impact on the test file:**
- The test file uses `enzyme`'s `shallow` rendering and only tests the `parseValue` method on the component instance. It doesn't query for tooltip content, doesn't use `getByText`/`getByRole`, doesn't interact with tooltips at all.
- The tests are purely logic tests (parsing find/hide values into selectors). They won't be affected by Tooltip's portal change.

**Conclusion:** No changes are needed to either file. The Tooltip behavioral change (Popper appendTo default) doesn't affect this code because:
1. The main file uses Tooltip in a standard way that works fine with portal rendering
2. The test file doesn't test Tooltip rendering or query tooltip content at all

## Changes Applied

**No changes were necessary.** The migration rule flags a transitive behavioral change in Tooltip (Popper's `appendTo` default changed from inline to `document.body` portal). However:

1. **Main file**: The three `<Tooltip>` usages (lines 336, 379, 406) are simple hover tooltips wrapping buttons. The portal rendering change doesn't affect their production functionality — tooltips are overlay components that work correctly (often better) with portal rendering.

2. **Test file**: Uses Enzyme `shallow` rendering and only tests the `parseValue()` method's logic (selector parsing). No tooltip content is queried, no DOM assertions are made on tooltip elements, so the behavioral change has zero impact on tests.
